output_debit_classifier.reason: This transaction represents the revenue recognition event for a service that was prepaid by the customer. When the deposit was originally received, it was recorded as a liability (Unearned Revenue or Customer Deposits) because the company had not yet earned it—there was a performance obligation outstanding. Now that the service has been delivered, the performance obligation is satisfied under IFRS 15, and the revenue can be recognized. The entry decreases the liability (debit Unearned Revenue) and increases revenue (credit Service Revenue) for the $100 amount. No cash changes hands in this transaction since payment was received earlier. No tax is mentioned in the transaction text, so no tax lines are added.

The deposit was recorded as a liability when received; now that the performance obligation is satisfied, we convert the liability to earned revenue.
